2C69C22C6654E68B76E0EC8B2FDB53AE: Unsealed inputs found in Area {0}, will auto-isolate them
9835443E53C4288A55AD0BEBFB027EBB: This trigger condition will be true while the selected storage unit is in the secure pending state, due to it having one or more unsealed inputs.
353C2893E28BD0EFDE5B5D2BA7316B6D: This trigger condition will be true while there are one or more storage units in the chosen storage block that are pending secure due to unsealed inputs.
1774DEE69BB4F87ACCA6CD2178517F6C: - An area arm attempt by a user that fails due to unsealed inputs will result in a Failed to Close event being reported.
5489D0965F9FDEA2C971381B503159BB: Auto-Isolate Unsealed Inputs on User Arm
9F9870BB94DDB75AB2AAE92592CFBE65: If enabled, the system will automatically isolate unsealed inputs in this area during the pre-arm test, rather than failing the arm if armed via a reader or a remote, or prompting a user to seal or isolate them if arming from an LCD Terminal / web interface. These inputs will remain isolated until either the input returns to a secure state, or the area is disarmed.
724FC3CF975687ADC976B2C149AB18F7: Auto-Isolate Unsealed Exit Path Inputs on Exit
65885EB813C629EBF9F3CA3FD184C694: Auto-Isolate Unsealed Exit Path Inputs On Exit
06993C6D024A010A758208CE840FA6B2: -  If selected, the input's alarm/active state is swapped with the sealed/inactive state. This is used when a normally-open detection device, such as a smoke detector, is wired to the system. A normally-open device reverses the End of Line resistance and will appear sealed when in alarm and in alarm when sealed.
12BDF7BA40E5C1D817061AAFFE1EADC8: - Any arm attempts that failed to arm the area will be notified. The message will include which user attempt the arm, which area was being armed and why the arm failed (either due to unsealed inputs or no permissions)
F0337D9ED24B7859949AA097621AE22A: page. The arm request will fail if the user does not have permission to arm the area, or if there are unsealed inputs in that area.
90D470E53447DFEEDE27D8996725F648: - this will arm every area in the system that the user has permission to arm. The arm request will fail if the user does not have permission to arm any areas, or one of the areas they can control contains unsealed inputs.
90E6EA19833F47C92FC4D58646E78B88: If an arm request fails, the user can attempt to find which input was unsealed and fix it, or try to arm via an LCD Terminal or the web interface which will provide additional information for why the arm failed.
2A8671A5874D6E6033A160F443FEF8B9: -  If the door is held open for too long the reader will make a beeping sound until the door reed or tongue sense are sealed. DOTL functionality is configured in the
1E18567AC0CF32ABCC1117D8B378AF69: - When the area fails to arm, either due to permissions or unsealed inputs, the SIFER reader will sound the Area Arm Failure tone and the Big LED will flash red.
284970FC0A6DE23ABD4F16A5A7BB1A34: If the option is enabled, the Unit alarm state will be cleared as soon as all inputs within the secure unit are sealed or when the unit is unlocked. This allows the unit alarm state to follow the state of its inputs, but has the consequence that if a unit only has a door sensor then someone could open a secure unit door, enter, then close it straight away, and the unit would no longer be in alarm.
80F7FF7FD21904D026C7F5DDFF5A4902: The End of Line configuration such as 2k2 + 6k8 determines the resistance values for the seal and alarm states. The first number 2k2 is the resistance that should represent a sealed input. The sum of both numbers 2k2 + 6k8 = 9k is the resistance for an input in alarm.
6780A9253A52CC182666E94EFB0AF6A4: Most security systems use End of Line resistors to determine if an input is in the alarm, tamper or sealed state. Inner Range products have historically used 2k2 and 6k8 or 2k2 and 2k2 ohm resistor pairs, however the Inception Controller and expansion modules can be configured to use other End of Line resistance values. This allows a security system from a different manufacturer to be easily replaced by an Inception Controller without changing the End of Line resistors found on every detection device.
B23C7F1B6241ECBB4EC0BAF7DF0E573E: - The tamper switch is open circuit when sealed/closed.
19CDB402E83BD4E96875FE6F0E552DFA: - The tamper switch is closed circuit when sealed/closed.
8EA3D75A79AA464B9CF267CEDFBC4CC6: Readers can be configured to arm one or more areas when a user presents their card at the reader three times in a row in short succession. A user can only arm areas that they have permission to arm and the arm may fail if the area contains unsealed inputs. If this occurs, the user should use a more advanced interface to try and arm, such as the LCD Terminal or the web interface.
6B00437572F948B1121913A0A501428A: Area Arm Failed due to Unsealed Inputs
